route.name: The Baldy Bruiser
route.region: Big Bear / Mount Baldy / San Gabriel Mountains
route.description: Public GPX Southern California mountain traverse with huge climbing, dirt roads, trail pieces, pavement connectors, and point-to-point logistics.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX Southern California mountain traverse with huge climbing, dirt roads, trail pieces, pavement connectors, and point-to-point logistics.
route.bestSeason: Cooler shoulder seasons; source guidance and July heat make the full route a poor next-weekend fit.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX Southern California mountain traverse with huge climbing, dirt roads, trail pieces, pavement connectors, and point-to-point logistics.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Mountain water, ranger-station/business refills, and long dry sections must be checked from the GPX and current sources.
resources.food: Big Bear and foothill services are key anchors; confirm hours and route access.
resources.camping: National Forest camping/lodging sequence must be verified route-day by route-day before reconsideration.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Keep as a future hard-route idea.
Do not promote for July 10-13 without a shortened GPX segment.
Verify transit and forest closures before any serious plan.
hazards: too long for a casual weekend

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Current July 10-13 screening blocks gate confirmation. Keep as planning/reference only.
No official overlay yet for Angeles NF plus San Bernardino NF closures along the raw GPX.
San Bernardino NF active closures and fire restrictions may affect the route.
The full route is too long/hard for a casual July 10-13 weekend and needs a shortened segment before serious vetting.
requiredFixes: Resolve the July-window blocker or choose a different route.
Overlay GPX against Angeles NF and San Bernardino NF active forest orders, especially Line Fire, Deep Creek, waterways, and MT/FC road closures.
Select legal developed/allowed overnight locations and verify fire-stove compliance.
Refresh CAL FIRE, InciWeb, Angeles/San Bernardino alerts, AQI/smoke, and heat forecast before any promotion.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Too long and too hot for the July 10-13 weekend; keep as a cooler-season or aggressively shortened rework candidate.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Angeles NF High Fire Restrictions and San Bernardino NF Fire Restrictions are active; San Bernardino also lists Line Fire Area Closure, Deep Creek Area Closure, road closures, and waterways closure that must be checked against the GPX.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: Raw Big Bear-to-LA GPX crosses Angeles and San Bernardino NF corridors with multiple active forest orders/closures requiring official segment overlay.

days.name: The Baldy Bruiser segment 1
days.camp: National Forest camping/lodging sequence must be verified route-day by route-day before reconsideration.

waypoints.name: Cajon - gas station resupply - Circle K, Subway, McD's etc...
Camp Williams - water, camping, cafe Thurs-Fri during winter
Road officially closed road until Sept 2017
West Fork Campsite/good stream water from here onwards
Some steep/rocky/bouldery sections along this section... including the Devil's Staircase!
Electrical plugs to the side of building.
